rulings.Case Title: ROBERT KACSUTA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 8, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 6. Defendant Schneider Electric USA, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs presented insufficient evidence to create a triable issue regarding asbestos content of any product attributable to defendant that Mr. Kacsuta was exposed to. Defendant¿s objections to Ken Cohen¿s declaration are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH FERNANDEZ VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 8, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Defendant Sequoia Ventures Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of production that plaintiffs do not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Fernandez was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s failure to file and serve on plaintiffs its declaration and supporting evidence precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JUAN CRUZ HINOJOSA et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Specially Appearing Defendant Tingue, Brown Co.S Demurrer To Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint For Wrongful Death And Survival - Asbestos
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 8, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2. Defendant Hoffman/New Yorker, Inc.¿s joinder in Tingue Brown & Co.¿s demurrer to the second amended complaint is off calendar as moot given the ruling on the underlying demurrer. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JUAN CRUZ HINOJOSA et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 8, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2. Defendant Hoffman/New Yorker, Inc.¿s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs¿ second amended complaint is granted with 10 days leave to amend. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JUAN CRUZ HINOJOSA et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 8, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2. Specially appearing defendant Tingue Brown & Co.¿s demurrer to the second amended complaint is sustained with leave to amend. The general demurrer as to the entire complaint is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to give plaintiffs the opportunity, if they are able to do so, to put defendants on notice of who is bringing the action. The demurrer as to the claims of Rebecca Cruz Marquet, Maria Dolores Cruz Miller, Sandra Patricia Cruz Miller, Artemisa Cruz Miller and Adriana Cruz Medina is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to give plaintiffs the opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to demonstrate that they have the right to bring a wrongful death action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES SCHNEBLE VS. COLTEC INDUSTRIES, INC.
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 8, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Defendant Flowserve Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden but plaintiff¿s declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS METCALF et al VS. PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 8, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4. Defendant Parker-Hannifin Corp.¿s motion to admit Jeffrey M. Wolf to appear as counsel pro hac vice is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CASEY, PATRICIA AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR, AND AS et al VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 8, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs¿ complaint is granted in part and off calendar in part. Defendant¿s motion is granted with 10 days leave to amend as to any damages alleged pursuant to CCP 377.34, as those are not allowed in a wrongful death action. (CCP 377.61.) Defendant¿s motion is off calendar as to punitive damages as plaintiffs have dismissed that claim. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CASEY, PATRICIA AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR, AND AS et al VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 8, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s demurrer to plaintiffs¿ complaint is overruled in part, sustained in part and off calendar in part. Defendant¿s demurrer as to the fifth (negligence-wrongful death) and sixth (strict liability-wrongful death) causes of action is overruled because those causes of action do not involve the same primary right as the causes of action from the personal injury action and thus CCP 430.10(c) is inapplicable. Defendant¿s demurrer as to the third cause of action (false representation) is sustained with 10 days leave to amend pursuant to CCP 430.10(c). Defendant¿s demurrer to the first (negligence-survival) and second (strict liability-survival) causes of action and plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages is off calendar because plaintiffs have dismissed them. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)
